channel.title: Cli on davegallant.ca
channel.description: Recent content in Cli on davegallant.ca

item.title: pvectl: A CLI for Managing Proxmox
item.description: My home Proxmox cluster has grown into two nodes, a few dozen LXC containers, and a handful of VMs. Recently I’ve been exploring ways to make this experience more streamlined, especially when creating new containers, shelling into containers, running migrations, restoring from backups, etc. Usually when I need to do this, I either SSH into one of the proxmox nodes or open up the GUI. When on a node, I usually run pct or qm, and then immediately run into the issue: which ID was the thing I actually wanted? Which node was it running on?
